scenario: A team migrates 200 instances of order-v3 to order-v4 using Operate's Process Instance Migration feature. After applying the migration, 195 instances succeeded and 5 failed. The team needs to see which 5 and why.
question: Which Operate view shows the per-instance migration results?

options.text: md:13 20 ("On the batch operation details page, expand a failed item row to view the failure reason"). Typical failure reasons include unmapped flow nodes, variable type mismatch, or token at a position the target plan doesn't cover.

Tasklist -- navigate to the User Tasks list and look for tasks that appeared or were cancelled as a result of the migration, using this as a proxy for understanding which instances failed. Tasklist is the end-user interface for completing assigned work items; it has no view of batch operations, migration results, or process instance migration history. Failed migration instances would not surface as Tasklist events since the migration failure occurs at the process structural level before any tasks are activated.

Optimize -- look in the process analytics dashboard for any anomalies in the migrated instances' performance metrics or duration data as indicators of migration failures. Optimize is the historical analytics platform for process performance insights; it does not track operational results of batch migration commands or expose per-instance migration failure reasons. Migration results are operational data managed by Operate, not analytical data surfaced by Optimize.

Build a custom monitoring dashboard that queries the Orchestration REST API for each of the 200 instance IDs, checks their current state, and compares them against the expected target process version to identify the 5 that failed migration. While building a custom solution is possible, it is entirely unnecessary: Operate's built-in Batch Operations page already provides exactly this information -- per-instance migration status and failure reasons -- accessible with a few UI clicks and without any custom development.

a.text: Correct. Operate tracks batch operations (cancel, retry, modify, migrate) on the dedicated Batch Operations page (top nav: Operations > Batch operations per monitor-batch-operations.md:15). Each batch entry links to a batch operation details page that shows per-instance status; expanding any failed-item row reveals the failure reason (:20). After a 200-instance migration with 5 failures, the team opens the batch detail, expands the 5 failed rows for specific reasons, addresses each (typically by adjusting the migration plan and retrying the failed subset via Selections). Authorization to view batch ops is governed by READ on BATCH_OPERATIONS (per operate/overview/access-control.md).

b.text: Incorrect. Tasklist handles User Tasks, not batch op results.

c.text: Incorrect. Optimize is analytics, not operations.

explanation: Operate tracks batch operations (cancel, retry, modify, migrate) on the dedicated Batch Operations page (top nav: Operations > Batch operations per monitor-batch-operations.md:15). Each batch entry links to a batch operation details page that shows per-instance status; expanding any failed-item row reveals the failure reason (:20). After a 200-instance migration with 5 failures, the team opens the batch detail, expands the 5 failed rows for specific reasons, addresses each (typically by adjusting the migration plan and retrying the failed subset via Selections). Authorization to view batch ops is governed by READ on BATCH_OPERATIONS (per operate/overview/access-control.md).
